cases.saccharine: excessively sweet or sentimental in a sickly way
cases.non sequitur: a statement that does not logically follow from what preceded it
cases.formulaic: produced in a mechanical, predictable way according to a fixed pattern
cases.avert: to turn away, especially one's eyes, or to prevent something bad
cases.rein in: to restrain or keep control over someone or something
cases.serendipity: the occurrence of finding pleasant things by chance
cases.concoct: to make something by combining ingredients or ideas, often deceptively
cases.ford: to cross a river or stream at a shallow point
cases.je ne sais quoi: an indefinable, elusive quality that makes something distinctive
cases.fortitude: courage and strength in facing pain or adversity
cases.unfilial: not showing the respect or duty expected toward one's parents
cases.smart aleck: an obnoxiously self-assured and cocky person
cases.entrance: to fill someone with delight, captivating their attention completely
cases.spinster: an unmarried woman, especially an older one
cases.unsolicited: not asked for, given without being requested
cases.astute: having sharp judgment, shrewd and perceptive
cases.slapstick: boisterous comedy relying on exaggerated physical actions
cases.atrophy: to waste away or decline from disuse
cases.prerogative: a right or privilege exclusive to a particular person
cases.dispassionate: not influenced by strong emotion, impartial
cases.guileless: innocent and honest, without deception or cunning
cases.rake over the coals: to severely criticize or reprimand someone
cases.sully: to damage the purity or integrity of something, to tarnish
cases.fledgling: a person or organization that is new and inexperienced
cases.chagrin: a feeling of annoyance or embarrassment at having failed
cases.poignant: evoking a keen sense of sadness or regret
cases.impugn: to dispute the truth, validity, or honesty of something
cases.spry: active and lively, especially of an older person
cases.avant-garde: new and experimental ideas, especially in the arts
cases.lull: to soothe someone into a state of false security or calm
cases.paragon: a model of excellence or perfection
cases.boisterous: noisy, energetic, and cheerful in an unrestrained way
cases.hooligan: a violent troublemaker, especially a young one
cases.troglodyte: a person who is regarded as being deliberately ignorant or old-fashioned
cases.austere: severely simple and plain, without adornment
cases.tumultuous: marked by confusion, disorder, or chaotic upheaval
cases.resilient: able to recover quickly from difficulties, tough
cases.gag order: a legal or official order prohibiting public discussion of a matter
cases.rumormonger: a person who spreads rumors, especially malicious ones
